fixture.description: A new alternate constructor `NewBoundedPool` is added to the worker-pool `Pool` struct but, unlike the existing `NewPool`, it never initializes the `counters map[string]int` field with make(), leaving it nil. The unchanged `Record` method writes to that map (`p.counters[task]++`). Writing to a nil map panics at runtime with "assignment to entry in nil map", so any Pool built via NewBoundedPool crashes the first time Record is called. The read path (`Total`, `return p.counters[task]`) is safe on a nil map, which masks the bug in read-only tests. Fix: initialize the map (e.g. `counters: make(map[string]int)`) in NewBoundedPool (or before the first write).

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report, in its MAIN findings (not an appendix or a dropped/out-of-scope list), flags that the newly added `NewBoundedPool` constructor in internal/worker/pool.go leaves the `counters` map nil (it never calls make(), unlike NewPool), so when `Record` does `p.counters[task]++` it writes to a nil map and panics at runtime ("assignment to entry in nil map") — crashing any Pool built via NewBoundedPool on the first Record call. It must recommend initializing the map (e.g. `counters: make(map[string]int)` in NewBoundedPool, or initializing before the first write). Severity must be CRITICAL or higher. It is fine (not required) if the report also notes that reads via Total are safe on a nil map and thus hide the bug. FAIL if this nil-map-write panic is absent, reported below CRITICAL (e.g. IMPORTANT/SUGGESTED), only in an appendix, attributed to the wrong file, dropped as pre-existing/out-of-scope, or if the report merely mentions the map without identifying that NewBoundedPool's missing make() causes a runtime panic on write.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags that NewBoundedPool leaves the counters map nil (no make()) while Record writes to it
Identifies the runtime panic 'assignment to entry in nil map' on the first Record call for a Pool built via NewBoundedPool
Recommends initializing the map with make(map[string]int) in NewBoundedPool (or before the first write)
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT assume NewBoundedPool initializes counters elsewhere (it does not; the field is left nil on that path)
Does NOT claim the read path (Total) is the bug — reads from a nil map are safe
Does NOT drop the finding as pre-existing or out-of-scope (the nil-construction path is introduced by this diff)
Does NOT downgrade the panic to a style/lint nit or below CRITICAL
